description: Document the missing opentabs stop command and --background flag for opentabs start in the CLI reference page. These are fully implemented features that are completely absent from the docs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add --background flag to opentabs start and document opentabs stop
userStories.description: As a user, I want the CLI reference to document the --background flag for opentabs start and the opentabs stop command so that I can run the server as a background daemon.
acceptanceCriteria: The opentabs start options table includes a row for `--background` with description explaining it starts the server in the background, writes a PID file to ~/.opentabs/server.pid, and exits immediately
A new `## opentabs stop` section exists after `opentabs start`, with command syntax `opentabs stop [options]`
The opentabs stop section has an options table with `--port <number>` (default 9515, used as fallback if no PID file found)
The opentabs stop section explains the shutdown behavior: reads PID from ~/.opentabs/server.pid, sends SIGTERM, waits up to 5 seconds, cleans up stale PID files
The opentabs start section mentions that when using --background, use opentabs stop to stop the server
The lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx

1. Add `--background` to the opentabs start options table (around line 62-67). The source is at platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts line 343: `.option('--background', 'Start the server in the background')`. When used, the server detaches, writes PID to ~/.opentabs/server.pid via getPidFilePath(), and the CLI exits immediately.

2. Add a new `## opentabs stop` section right after the opentabs start section (after line 79). The source is at platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts. The command:
   - Reads PID from ~/.opentabs/server.pid if the file exists
   - Sends SIGTERM to the process
   - Waits up to 5 seconds for exit
   - Falls back to health-check probe on the configured port if no PID file
   - Cleans up stale PID files
   - Has one option: `--port <number>` (default 9515)

Follow the same Table component patterns used by other command sections in this file. Look at how opentabs start or opentabs status sections are structured and follow the same pattern.
